rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. SHIMMICK CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdrawal Swiss Re'S Answer-In Intervention
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 11, 2026, Line 1, INTERVENOR SWISS RE ON BEHALF OF CROSS-DEFENDANT GEO GROUT, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Withdrawal Swiss Re'S Answer-In Intervention. The unopposed motion to withdraw Swiss Re's answer in intervention is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(301/CVA) The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CHANG H. AHN ET AL VS. BUCHALTER, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Clarify Judgment By Attaching Final Arbitration Award And Confirmed By The Court To The Final Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc And Entering Judgment Effective February 18, 2025; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 11, 2026, Line 2, DEFENDANT BUCHALTER, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Clarify Judgment By Attaching Final Arbitration Award And Confirmed By The Court To The Final Judgment Nunc Pro Tunc And Entering Judgment Effective February 18, 2025. The motion is granted. The court cannot locate a document in the register of actions that is entitled a judgment, and it appears that both parties proceeded as if the court's order granting the petition confirm arbitration were a judgment. It is appropriate for a judgment to be entered on the arbitration award nunc pro tunc, given that all parties proceeded as if the award were embodied in a judgment. Judgment creditor is ordered to submit a proposed amended judgment to the court and serve it on the judgment debtor. The judgment debtor may submit any objections to the proposed judgment pursuant to Rule of Court 3.1590, subdivision (j). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HSU VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 11, 2026, Line 5, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions. Defendant the Regents of the University of California's motion for terminating sanctions is continued for hearing to May 26, 2026. The court orders further responses by Hsu prior to the continued hearing date. The court continues this motion for hearing to May 26, 2026. The court orders Hsu to make further responses to the following discovery that were omitted from her January 28, 2026 amendments: form interrogatory (general) nos. 12.6, 12.7, 13.1, 13.2, and 17.1; form interrogatory (employment) nos. 200.5, 204.1, 204.6, 210.6, 212.4, 212.5, and 212.7; special interrogatory no. 44; and request for production of document nos. 28-34. In addition, the court orders Hsu to further amend her deficient responses to form interrogatory nos. 12.3, 200.1, 200.2, 200.3, 214.1; special interrogatory no. 42; request for production nos. 1, 4-7, 10, 14, 18, 21, 25, and 27 to address each issue the court has identified in this order. Hsu shall make the amendments ordered herein by May 19, 2026. On that date, she shall file with the court a declaration (1) attaching her amended responses, which must be verified; and (2) attaching proof of payment of the $6000 sanction. The court is inclined to grant further monetary sanctions to the Regents but reserves decision on the sanction, including whether issue, evidentiary, or terminating sanctions are appropriate, based on Hsu's showing at the continued hearing. (The Court's complete tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Housing Authority Of The City And County Of San Francisco?S Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Williams And Plaintiff Watson For Failure To Follow Discovery Orders; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: On the Law & Motion/Discovery calendar for Monday, May 11, 2026, Line 6, DEFENDANT HOUSING AUTHORITY OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY 1815 EGBERT AVE'S Motion For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Williams And Plaintiff Watson For Failure To Follow Discovery Orders. Defendant the San Francisco Housing Authority's motion for sanctions is denied without prejudice. The Housing Authority seeks sanctions for plaintiffs Andre Williams and Timojene Watson's failures to comply with the court's prior discovery orders. Code of Civil Procedure, section 2023.040, states: "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought, and specify the type of sanction sought. . . ." The Housing Authority's notice of motion states only that it seeks an order "sanctioning" plaintiffs and does not specify the type of sanction sought. The Housing Authority's motion is procedurally deficient, and it is therefore denied without prejudice. Nothing in this order should be taken to suggest that any party may avoid full compliance with court orders. The court also observes that Plaintiff's opposition contains the following statement: " 'Sanctions should not be imposed where a party has made a good faith effort to comply with discovery obligations.' -Diepenbrock v. Brown (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 743." The quoted language does not appear in Diepenbrock, which reversed a sanctions order because "conflicting legal authority on an unsettled issue provided substantial justification for [the sanctioned party's] position." (Diepenbrock v. Brown (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 743, 749.) The court cautions plaintiffs that it is misleading to the court for them to quote cases that do not contain the quoted language. Self-represented litigants have the same duty not to mislead the court as attorneys. (Kobayashi v. Superior Court (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 536, 543.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
